steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map the passage: sentence 1 introduces a characteristic shared by all baleen whales (krill-filtering via baleen plates). Sentence 2 extends that shared trait (30% body mass per day). Sentence 3 contrasts the humpback and fin whale in size — a difference directly tied to how much each consumes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It draws a distinction between the sizes of the humpback whale and the fin whale and then presents a feature that they have in common.’: The passage moves in the opposite direction: shared feature first, then the distinction. The sequence is reversed. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It details a relationship between humpback and fin whales and krill and then discusses an unexpected consequence of a change in baleen whale populations.’: The passage never mentions population changes or consequences of population shifts. This content is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It discusses a unique feature of the humpback whale and how the lack of that feature in the fin whale explains differences in the two species' behavior.’: Baleen-plate feeding is not unique to humpbacks — the passage explicitly states it applies to "all species of baleen whales." ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It describes a characteristic shared by all baleen whales and then illustrates a difference between the humpback whale and the fin whale that is relevant to that characteristic.’: The shared characteristic is krill consumption; the size difference between humpback and fin whale directly illustrates how much each can consume. Sequence and relevance both match. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
